pi_items.verified_value: food.gdt from Hill, Griffiths, Lim Principles of Econometrics 5th Edition (POE5)
pi_items.source: Web search: principlesofeconometrics.com, gretl documentation, setup_gretl.sh references /opt/gretl_data/poe5/food.gdt
Task description specifies income=20 as structural break point; verifier.py uses income_over_20 = max(0, income - 20); regression tests spline at income > 20

Sum of income coefficient (13.5392) and income_over_20 coefficient (-6.0515) = 7.4878; represents marginal effect for income > 20

privileged_info_summary: The food.gdt dataset is a real, publicly available dataset from Hill, Griffiths, and Lim's Principles of Econometrics (5th Edition) containing 40 household observations of food expenditure (dependent variable) and income (independent variable). The task performs piecewise linear regression with a structural break (spline knot) at income=20. The correct OLS coefficients for the spline regression are: constant=67.0486, income=13.5392, income_over_20=-6.0515. The marginal propensity to consume for households with income>20 (high_income_slope) equals 7.4878 (sum of income and income_over_20 coefficients). The data is non-synthetic and sourced from an established econometrics teaching textbook.
